title: hutera cheiranthos subsp. nevadensis
intro: Hutera cheiranthos subsp. nevadensis, also known as Nevada wallflower or Sierra wallflower, is a rare, alpine wildflower native to the Sierra Nevada mountain range in California. This plant contains sanguinarine, a bioactive alkaloid with medicinal properties, which has been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Hutera cheiranthos subsp. nevadensis include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it a potential remedy for wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tincture or infused oil for use in herbal medicine, although it's essential to exercise caution and consult with a medical professional before using it due to its potential toxicity.
